# Security Policy

## Reporting A Vulnerability

Do not report suspected vulnerabilities in a public issue, discussion, pull request, or chat transcript.

After M0 enables it, use [GitHub private vulnerability reporting](https://github.com/alex-alecu/vault-desk/security/advisories/new) to submit:

- A concise description of the vulnerability and affected boundary.
- Reproduction steps or a minimal proof of concept.
- Expected impact and affected platforms or versions.
- Suggested mitigation, if known.

Do not include customer documents, live credentials, unrelated personal information, or destructive payloads. Use synthetic fixtures and redact local paths and identifiers when possible.

If private reporting is not yet available during the documentation-only phase, do not publish sensitive details. Open a public issue containing only a request for private security contact and enough non-sensitive context for the maintainer to respond.

Vault Desk has no released implementation or supported version while the repository remains documentation-only. Reports about the planned architecture are still welcome when they identify a concrete flaw in a documented security boundary.

The maintainer will acknowledge the report privately, assess severity and scope, coordinate remediation and verification, and publish details only after a safe disclosure point is agreed.
